content.problemStatement: You are given a sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. Find the maximum value of the expression ∣Ax−Ay∣+∣Ay−Az∣+∣Az−Ax∣|A_x-A_y| + |A_y-A_z| + |A_z-A_x|∣Ax​−Ay​∣+∣Ay​−Az​∣+∣Az​−Ax​∣ over all triples of pairwise distinct valid indices (x,y,z)(x, y, z)(x,y,z).
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the maximum value of ∣Ax−Ay∣+∣Ay−Az∣+∣Az−Ax∣|A_x-A_y| + |A_y-A_z| + |A_z-A_x|∣Ax​−Ay​∣+∣Ay​−Az​∣+∣Az​−Ax​∣.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The value of the expression is always 101010. For example, let x=1x = 1x=1, y=2y = 2y=2 and z=3z = 3z=3, then it is ∣2−7∣+∣7−5∣+∣5−2∣=5+2+3=10|2 - 7| + |7 - 5| + |5 - 2| = 5 + 2 + 3 = 10∣2−7∣+∣7−5∣+∣5−2∣=5+2+3=10.
Example case 2: Since all values in the sequence are the same, the value of the expression is always 000.
Example case 3: One optimal solution is x=1x = 1x=1, y=2y=2y=2 and z=5z = 5z=5, which gives ∣2−2∣+∣2−5∣+∣5−2∣=0+3+3=6|2 - 2| + |2 - 5| + |5 - 2| = 0 + 3 + 3 = 6∣2−2∣+∣2−5∣+∣5−2∣=0+3+3=6.
